drafts.qrt_text: clicky is the vibe coding equivalent for learning -- ai that sits next to your cursor and shows you how. next-level learning tool.
claude mythos preview -- anthropic moving fast. opus 4.6 was just two months ago.
finally a claude model that actually watches the videos it cites. the ai citation problem just got a little more real.
I counted 47 Chainlink prediction 2027 articles this week. None had a date. None had a methodology. Just AI scraping AI, calling it analysis.

Your AI agent just spent $0.02 on an API call. No credit card. No approval flow. Just paid. x402 hit $600M annual volume. AI agents buying things on their own now, not soon. This changes the engineer role entirely.
the ai agent advice no one gives: dont start by automating your hardest task. automate the boring stuff first -- emails, followups, templated replies. build the muscle before the marathon.
Ant Group just dropped Anvita. Lets AI agents autonomously hold, trade, and settle payments in stablecoins. No humans needed. The agentic finance era did not wait for 2030.
n8n went from Zapier alternative to visual AI workflow engine. Chain LLM calls, vector DB queries, and tool-using agents, no code required. The no-code agent space is getting real.
67k+ software engineering jobs open right now. Highest in 3+ years, up 30% YTD. The narrative that AI is replacing engineers? Not backed by the data, at least not yet.
the model does not hear your question. it only sees what you show it.
lessons in what matters: time or money. never both. (91yo building w/ Claude: vibe coding spread to non-programmers now)
the hard part is knowing which variable matters. IBM says 2025 is the year of agents, but theres nuance in every claim.
The billionaire test: if you cant explain your idea to a busy founder in 30 seconds, its not ready yet. Use it to pressure-check pitch decks.
leaks work better than docs.
the anti-bot arms race just started. camofox dropped free, cloudflare sees through it anyway.
the personality matters more than the prompts. USC study: people like extroverted robots but relate to neurotic ones.
Agents will be your primary customer within 24 months. Thats the timeline Alchemy just put on the table. No more eventually. Theyre building the payment rails now.
all agents should have cute personalities. the penguin angle is genius.
Whale.io just launched the first AI Agent MCP for crypto casino. Agents now gamble autonomously. We really doing this?
Ant Group just launched Anvita. A platform where AI agents, not humans, are the main crypto traders. Agents with wallets, autonomous spending. The infrastructure for agent-to-agent commerce is here.

AI agents are no longer just analyzing markets. Ant Groups Anvita lets them actually trade, holding assets, paying each other via x402/USDC. The distinction between AI analyst and AI trader just collapsed.
x402 payment protocol hit $600M annualized volume -- AI agents autonomously paying for API calls. The engineer role shifts from "provisioner" to "funder".
Perplexity just dropped an AI that drafts your actual tax return. 25% of workers now plan to use AI for filing -- up from 11% last year. The $15B tax prep industry just got disrupted.
Crypto devs building AI agents face $1K-$5K/day bills. Anthropic just cracked down on heavy users. The cost of agentic AI is becoming the bottleneck.
The agent wrapper era is ending. The founders who ship real utility, not wrappers, are the ones who will matter in 2026.

X now auto-locks accounts that mention crypto for the first time. The scam prevention logic is sound, but imagine being a dev who just learned about Bitcoin, locked out until you verify. The workaround will be glorious.
If an AI agent screws up your finances, who gets sued? Nobody. Thats the wild west of agentic AI in 2026.
